Restore /docs:pr command with cross-referencing support

Restore the /docs:pr command that creates documentation PRs in .docs/ with
automatic detection and cross-referencing to parent project PRs. The command
handles linting, PR creation, and bidirectional linking between documentation
and parent PRs.

relative time expressions into separate sections with detailed tables.

commit.message:
Document time function comprehensively

Add comprehensive documentation for the time() function including a table of all
supported formats (ISO 8601, Unix with @, relative times), detailed examples for
each format, and timezone handling. Reorganize the work-with-time guide to
introduce time() first, add "When to use which" guidance comparing time() vs
parse_time(), and restructure Unix timestamp and log parsing sections with
clearer examples. All outputs verified through the tenzir binary.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Document local plugin development workflow

Use --plugin-dir to load plugins directly from source, bypassing
the plugin cache. Removes enabledPlugins from settings to avoid
confusion between marketplace and local versions.

commit.message:
Add link validation for See Also components

The See Also components (Op, Fn, Guide, Tutorial, Explanation) generate
links dynamically, which starlight-links-validator couldn't check because
it runs before components render. A custom remark plugin now extracts
slot content and adds data-href attributes that the validator can check.

Also fixes two broken links discovered during testing:
- summarize.mdx: remove non-existent analyze-zeek-logs tutorial
- unroll.mdx: change <Op>flatten</Op> to <Fn>flatten</Fn>

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Update minimal button variant to match Tertiary button spec

Align the minimal button variant with the Tenzir design system's
Tertiary button specification. Updates use neutral colors instead of
primary colors: neutral-800 text in light mode, neutral-200 in dark
mode, with appropriate neutral background colors for hover and active
states. Also removes unnecessary padding-inline property for proper
button padding.

Updates the changelog generation script to use the new minimal variant
for the RSS Feed button.

commit.message:
Add Atom/RSS feed generation for changelog

Generate per-project and unified Atom feeds from changelog data:
- Per-project feeds at /changelog/{project}.xml (20 latest entries)
- Unified feed at /changelog/feed.xml (50 latest entries)
- Structured HTML content with breaking changes, features, and other sections
- Author and PR metadata for each entry
- Feed autodiscovery link in page headers
- RSS feed links throughout changelog UI

Users can now subscribe to release updates via their favorite feed reader.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix duplicate output lines in sync-claude-plugins script

The script was printing both "processing..." and the result on separate
lines. Now uses TTY detection to show inline progress that gets overwritten
in terminals, while non-TTY output shows only the final result.

version 0.0.0, enabling the first release to use standard SemVer bump

Updated the plugin template and SemVer examples accordingly.

commit.message:
Add excalidraw plugin for diagram generation

Introduces the excalidraw:diagramming skill for creating valid .excalidraw JSON files. The plugin provides comprehensive reference documentation for all element types (shapes, text, arrows, lines, freedraw, images, frames, and custom polygons) and styling properties (colors, fill patterns, positioning, and grouping). All element properties and constants are derived directly from Excalidraw source code, ensuring generated files render correctly in excalidraw.com and VS Code extensions.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enable version bumping for initial releases with implicit 0.0.0 base

When no prior releases exist, version bump flags (--major, --minor,
--patch) now work by using an implicit 0.0.0 base version. This allows
users to create their first release with semantic versioning without
needing to specify an explicit version string.

- Modified _resolve_release_version() to return 0.0.0 as base when no
  releases exist
- Updated test_release_create_semver_bumps to verify --patch creates 0.0.1
- Added test_release_create_bump_from_implicit_zero to test --minor (0.1.0)
  and --major (1.0.0) scenarios

commit.message:
Add Excalidraw-to-SVG build integration

Authors can now reference .excalidraw source files directly in markdown
with ![alt](diagram.excalidraw). The build converts these to SVG and
inlines them into HTML for CSS dark mode support.

- Add generate-excalidraw-svgs.mjs script using @excalidraw/utils
- Add remark plugin to inline SVGs at parse time
- Add Excalidraw fonts (Virgil, Cascadia, Excalifont, ComicShanns)
- Add InlineSVG.astro component for ESM imports
- Gitignore generated *.excalidraw.svg files
- Remove old post-build inline-svgs.mjs
- Rename hand-crafted *.excalidraw.svg to *.svg
- Document workflow in contribution guide

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Fix text positioning formula documentation for containers

alignment-based centering within containers. Additionally,
clarified that the `label` property is for Excalidraw's
JavaScript API only—raw JSON files require separate elements
with `containerId` and `boundElements` references. Shape-specific
offsets for ellipses and diamonds are now documented.

commit.message:
Document bidirectional arrow binding in Excalidraw

Enhanced the arrow binding documentation with a complete example showing
how to connect shapes with arrows that move together when dragged. Clarified
that bindings are bidirectional—both the arrow's startBinding/endBinding and
the shape's boundElements must reference each other. Added sections explaining
arrow positioning rules (first point must be [0, 0]) and formulas for
calculating edge attachment points from fixedPoint ratios.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix polygon path closing instructions in Excalidraw documentation

Polygons in Excalidraw require explicitly including the first point again
at the end to close the path when created programmatically via JSON. The
documentation incorrectly stated paths auto-close. Updated all polygon
examples and point count reference table to reflect this requirement.

commit.message:
Add parser and OCSF mapping workflows to TQL plugin

Introduce two guided slash commands and a new skill for creating and
enhancing Tenzir packages. The `/tql:make_parser` command walks through
5 phases to create parsing pipelines from log samples with iterative
testing. The `/tql:make_ocsf_mapping` command adds OCSF-compliant
mappings to existing parsers. The new `tql:managing-packages` skill
provides package structure guidance and testing workflows.

commit.message:
Add explicit skill declarations for subagents

Subagents no longer inherit skills from the parent conversation.
All required skills must now be declared in the `skills:` frontmatter
field, including transitive dependencies from slash commands.

commit.message:
Add XML parsing functions (#5640)

## Summary

Introduce two new TQL functions for parsing XML-formatted data:

- **`parse_xml`**: Generic XML parsing with XPath-based element
selection, configurable attribute prefixes, namespace handling, and
depth limiting
- **`parse_winlog`**: Specialized Windows Event Log XML parsing that
handles `System` and `EventData` sections with smart output formatting:
  - Named `<Data Name="...">` elements → record fields
  - Unnamed `<Data>` elements → list output
  - Other elements (like `UserData`) → generic nested structure

Both functions use libexpat for efficient streaming XML parsing and
integrate with Tenzir's multi-series builder for schema inference.

## Examples

```tql
// Parse generic XML with XPath
from {xml: "<catalog><book id=\"1\"><title>Guide</title></book></catalog>"}
result = xml.parse_xml(xpath="//book")

// Parse Windows Event Log
from {xml: "<Event>...</Event>"}
event = xml.parse_winlog()
```

## Test plan

- [x] Integration tests for `parse_xml` (basic, xpath, attr_prefix,
namespaces)
- [x] Integration tests for `parse_winlog`
- [x] Microsoft ground truth tests (Events 4624, 4625, 4627, 4648, 4688
from Microsoft Security Auditing docs)
- [x] Build passes with libexpat dependency

## Related

- Documentation: https://github.com/tenzir/docs/pull/147

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Add key_attr option to parse_xml for attribute-keyed records

When key_attr is set (e.g., key_attr="name"), elements with that
attribute use the attribute value as the field name instead of the
element tag. This transforms:

  <KEY name="QID">10001</KEY>
  <KEY name="TITLE">SQL Injection</KEY>

Into: {QID: "10001", TITLE: "SQL Injection"}

This is common in formats like Qualys vulnerability reports where
data is stored in generic KEY elements with name attributes.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add depth tracking to XML parser

Prevent stack overflow from deeply nested XML by tracking nesting
depth during SAX parsing. The default limit is 256 levels, which
is sufficient for legitimate XML while protecting against malicious
input.

commit.message:
Skip changelog adder for bot-authored PRs

Automated syncs (like OCSF docs sync and schema update) are not human-authored
changes and were causing misleading changelog entries. This change skips the
changelog adder action for PRs created by bot accounts, ensuring only
human-authored changes generate changelog entries.

of tracking upstream commit SHAs via marker files. This prevents
unnecessary syncs when the ocsf-docs repository has infrastructure-only
changes that don't affect articles or FAQs.

The changelog-adder GitHub Action now uses tenzir-changelog to render PR
comments instead of manually parsing YAML frontmatter. This improves
formatting and fixes issues with missing created dates and hard-wrapped
body text. The action now also supports multiple changelog entries per PR,
allowing changes across several plugins to appear in a single comment.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Pre-commit OCSF references with automated updates

OCSF schema references and documentation articles are now pre-committed
instead of being lazily generated. Two GitHub Actions check daily for
new OCSF releases and documentation updates, automatically filing PRs
when changes are detected.

Changes:
- Add ocsf-schema-update.yml workflow for version tracking
- Add ocsf-docs-sync.yml workflow for article/FAQ syncing
- Add sync-docs.py using Anthropic SDK for article descriptions
- Remove lazy ensure-references.sh script
- Update SKILL.md with curated article descriptions
- Remove 30-attribute limit in generate-references.py
- Include OCSF 1.7.0 schema references and documentation

commit.message:
Fix diagram generation accuracy and validation

Improved documentation to prevent common diagram generation errors:
- Text width estimation increased from 0.6 to 0.65 factor to prevent
  horizontal clipping when rendering text in Excalidraw diagrams
- Hexagon polygon formula for consistent 60-degree angles regardless of
  element dimensions
- Established minimum spacing rules: 40px between siblings, 60px between
  rows, 40px frame padding, 20px arrow clearance
- Added label text verification to validation checklist to catch spelling
  and naming inconsistencies

Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Use artist sloppiness as default for arrows and polygons

Arrow and polygon examples now default to roughness: 1 (Artist style)
instead of roughness: 0 (Architect style), matching Excalidraw's native
hand-drawn appearance. This ensures generated diagrams have the
characteristic sketchy look rather than perfectly precise lines.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

commit.message:
Add proper typing to parse_winlog output (#5645)

## Summary

- Add proper typing to `parse_winlog` System fields according to the
Windows Event Log schema
- `EventID`, `Version`, `Level`, `Task`, `Opcode`, `EventRecordID`,
`ProcessID`, `ThreadID` are now `int64`
- `TimeCreated` is now a `time` timestamp (parsed from the `SystemTime`
attribute)
- `EventData` values use type inference via `data_unparsed()` (numbers
become integers, IPs become `ip`)
- The `-` sentinel value is preserved as a string per MS-WMLOG spec
("not applicable")

## Test plan

- [x] Updated all MSFT test expectations to reflect typed output
- [x] Verified tests pass with `tenzir-test`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Add proper typing to parse_winlog output

The System fields are now typed according to the Windows Event Log schema:
- EventID, Version, Level, Task, Opcode, EventRecordID → int64
- TimeCreated → time (parsed from SystemTime attribute)
- ProcessID, ThreadID in Execution → int64

EventData values use type inference via data_unparsed(), so numeric
values become integers and IP addresses become ip types.

The "-" sentinel value is preserved as a string to indicate "not
applicable" per MS-WMLOG spec.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve installation guide structure and clarity

Restructure installation methods with platform support callout and
clearer step-by-step procedures. Changes include: added supported
platforms tip (macOS Apple Silicon, Linux x86_64/ARM64), reorganized
Debian/RPM/Tarball sections into Steps components for clarity, simplified
uvx and tarball sections by removing redundant content, and renamed
"Linux" section to "Packages".

commit.message:
Document Tenzir binary detection in MCP server reference

The MCP server auto-detects the Tenzir binary in this order:
1. TENZIR_BINARY environment variable
2. Local tenzir installation
3. uvx tenzir (if uv is available)

commit.message:
Fix XML parsing examples to handle multi-line documents

Using read_lines for XML input splits documents at newlines, producing
invalid XML fragments. For multiple events, use read_delimited with
</Event> as separator. For single events, use read_all.

Event Logs integration guide with practical examples for filtering
and extracting event data.

  text labels instead)

commit.message:
Unify show command with release display modes

The show command now provides unified control for entry selection and
display format. Previously, separate `show` and `release notes` commands
had overlapping functionality with different mental models: `show`
displayed entries in a flat list, while `release notes` formatted them
as release documents.

The new design follows one principle: selection is always entry-centric,
and `--release` is a display modifier. The `--all` flag enables batch
export of all releases in a single invocation—particularly valuable for
documentation sync scripts that previously required invoking the tool
once per release.

Breaking changes:
- `release notes` command removed (use `show <version> --release -m`)
- `show` now accepts `--release`, `--all`, `--released-only` flags
- With `--release`, JSON output is always an array of release objects

Implements helper functions for reusable release rendering logic:
- _render_release_header() for consistent headers
- _show_entries_table_all() and _show_entries_table_release_mode()
  for table display with new flags
- _show_entries_card() and _show_entries_export_all() for card and
  export views
- _build_release_payload() and _render_markdown_release_block()
  for JSON and markdown generation

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Default to uvx tenzir for pipeline execution (#14)

## Summary

Pipeline execution now uses `uvx tenzir` by default, removing the
requirement to have Tenzir installed in PATH. This makes the MCP server
easier to use out of the box.

- Default command changed from `tenzir` to `uvx tenzir`
- Added `TENZIR_BINARY` environment variable support for override
- Docker image sets `TENZIR_BINARY=tenzir` to use the pre-installed
binary
- Removed `.env.example` (documented unused environment variables)

## Test plan

- [x] Verified `uvx tenzir` works locally with `version` pipeline
- [x] Linters pass

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Make changelog release command autonomous

The `/changelog:release` command now runs in a forked context with
autonomous decision-making. It infers the version bump type from
unreleased entries (or accepts an optional `bump` argument),
automatically generates release titles, and proceeds without user
confirmation. The separate `releaser` subagent has been removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

checkers, and formatters. Update discovery to check standard config
files (pyproject.toml, package.json, Makefile) rather than searching
CI workflows, keeping the step fast. Add skip clause if no checks
are configured.

commit.message:
Make git commit command autonomous

Enable /git:commit to run in a forked context with autonomous
decision-making. The command now automatically splits multiple
unrelated changes into separate commits without prompting the user,
and always creates new commits rather than amending unless
explicitly requested.

Remove the committer subagent since the command now handles
autonomous execution directly.

commit.message:
Update tenzir-ship usage for new bulk export API

Replace inefficient per-release API calls with bulk export feature
(uvx tenzir-ship show all --release --json). This combines unreleased
entries and all releases into a single efficient call, improving
changelog sync performance.

- Refactor sync-changelog.mjs: Replace readUnreleased + readReleases
  with readAllReleases that uses bulk export
- Fix scope token documentation: Replace deprecated - shorthand with
  proper scope tokens (unreleased, released, latest)
- Update examples in maintain-a-changelog.mdx and write-a-package
  tutorial to use current API patterns

commit.message:
Replace scope filtering flags with positional tokens

The show command now uses positional scope tokens (unreleased, released,
latest, all) instead of flags (--unreleased, --released, --latest, --all).
This provides clearer separation between scope (what to show) and
presentation (how to show it via --release flag).

**Breaking changes:**
- `show --unreleased` → `show unreleased`
- `show --released` → `show released`
- `show --latest` → `show latest`
- `show --all` → `show all` (or omit for default)
- `show --release` now shows all entries instead of latest; use
  `show latest --release` to restore previous behavior
- Removed select_all, select_released, select_unreleased from Python API

**Implementation improvements:**
- SCOPE_TOKENS changed from dict to set[Scope] for simplicity
- Fixed error message for `-` identifier
- Removed unused imports
- Fixed variable shadowing in _show_entries_table_all
- All views (table, card, markdown, json) now behave consistently

All 87 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Markdown table rendering and remove release notes command

Add mdformat-gfm dependency to enable proper GitHub Flavored Markdown
table formatting in release notes and entry descriptions. Previously,
tables were collapsed to a single line due to missing GFM support in
the normalize_markdown() function.

Remove the deprecated `release notes` command from the CLI and the
`release_notes()` method from the Python API. The unified `show`
command with `--release` flag provides the same functionality with
a simpler mental model (selection is entry-centric, display is
independent).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Convert --stats flag to stats command with adaptive views

Replace the global --stats flag with a dedicated `stats` subcommand that
automatically adapts its display based on project structure:

- Vertical view for single projects: organized card layout with Project,
  Releases, Entry Types, and Entry Status sections
- Table view for multi-module projects: compact comparison with emoji
  headers matching the vertical view structure

Features:
- Exponentially weighted cadence calculation (releases per month/year)
  with recent months weighted more heavily
- `--table` flag to force table view even for single projects
- `--json` flag for structured programmatic output
- Emoji headers (📛, 🔖, 📅, 🔢, 🔄, 💥, 🚀, 🔧, 🐞, Σ, 📦, ⏳) for
  consistent visual scanning across formats

Extract stats functionality into dedicated _stats.py module. Remove
--stats flag and handler from _core.py. Update tests for new command
behavior and views.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Convert modules command to --show-modules global option

Replace the standalone `modules` subcommand with a `--show-modules`
global option for better introspection workflows. The option more
clearly indicates this is an auxiliary discovery feature rather than
a core shipping command.

Also fix the PATH column in module discovery output to display clean
relative paths (e.g., plugins/brand/changelog) instead of paths with
../ prefixes (e.g., ../plugins/brand/changelog). This improves
readability when modules are discovered outside the parent directory.

- Remove modules_cmd from _validate.py
- Add --show-modules option to main CLI group in _core.py
- Simplify module path calculation in modules.py
- Update tests to use --show-modules instead of modules command
- Update documentation to reflect new interface

Resolves the modules command refactor in preparation for larger
tenzir-changelog → tenzir-ship rename project.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

clearly indicates this is an auxiliary discovery feature rather than
a core shipping command.

Also fix the PATH column in module discovery output to display clean
relative paths (e.g., plugins/brand/changelog) instead of paths with
../ prefixes (e.g., ../plugins/brand/changelog). This improves
readability when modules are discovered outside the parent directory.

- Remove modules_cmd from _validate.py
- Add --show-modules option to main CLI group in _core.py
- Simplify module path calculation in modules.py
- Update tests to use --show-modules instead of modules command
- Update documentation to reflect new interface

Resolves the modules command refactor in preparation for larger
tenzir-changelog → tenzir-ship rename project.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Move bundle job into main workflow

Run Deploy and Bundle in parallel after Build completes. This:
- Simplifies artifact access (same workflow)
- Removes workflow_run trigger complexity
- Enables parallel execution of Deploy and Bundle

Also switches single-file generation to use llms-txt output (proper
links) instead of source MDX files (unrendered <Op> components).

commit.message:
Add documentation bundle release workflow

Automatically release documentation bundles on every push to main,
published to the `latest` release with two formats:

- tenzir-docs.tar.gz: Structured markdown files for programmatic access
- tenzir-docs.md: Single file with proper heading hierarchy for AI/LLM use

Changes:
- Rename generate-docs-map.mjs to generate-docs.mjs with --map/--full modes
- Add bundle.yaml workflow to create releases after successful Build & Deploy
- Implement heading demotion to preserve doc structure in single-file output
- Include index.md rename workaround for starlight-llms-txt root page bug

The release assets overwrite on each push (no notification churn) and are
ready for MCP servers, RAG pipelines, and AI assistants.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Render pages as headings in sitemap

Modify the sitemap generation script to display each page as a heading at the
appropriate level, rather than as a bullet point. This makes the hierarchy
clearer: pages become H3/H4/etc headings with their internal sections as flat
bullet lists. Also remove the auto-generation notice and clean up extra blank
lines between sections.

commit.message:
Document internal tooling for AI memory (#5655)

## Summary

Add reference documentation for generic utilities in the Tenzir
codebase,
helping AI models discover existing implementations instead of
hand-rolling
their own.

**New reference files:**

- **utilities.md**: String manipulation, encoding, containers (flat_map,
lru_cache), iteration helpers (zip, enumerate), numerics, operator
mixins,
  scope guards
- **hashing.md**: Generic `hash()` API, available algorithms, extension
via
  `hash_append`
- **parser-combinators.md**: Parser combinator operators, built-in
parsers,
  semantic actions
- **generic-types.md**: Coroutine generators, SI/binary literals, glob
  patterns, chunks, concepts

Each document focuses on user-facing usage examples and points to
headers
rather than duplicating internal implementation details.

## Test Plan

- [ ] References follow existing documentation style
- [ ] Code examples are accurate
- [ ] SKILL.md links work correctly

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Replace detail::zip with std::views::zip

Remove the custom `detail::zip` and `detail::zip_equal` utilities
(417 lines) in favor of C++23's `std::views::zip` from <ranges>.

For call sites that used `detail::zip_equal`, add explicit size
assertions before the loop since `std::views::zip` stops at the
shortest range rather than asserting equal lengths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

tree 44113f142c82107ff57f349c15f2d0011bc88dc5
parent 541a157e051bd392182c730a080599b2bbf28439
author Matthias Vallentin <matthias@vallentin.net> 1768305138 +0100

Replace detail::zip with std::views::zip

Remove the custom `detail::zip` and `detail::zip_equal` utilities
(417 lines) in favor of C++23's `std::views::zip` from <ranges>.

For call sites that used `detail::zip_equal`, add explicit size
assertions before the loop since `std::views::zip` stops at the
shortest range rather than asserting equal lengths.

commit.message:
Strip internal OCSF links to fix CI link validation

The OCSF schema descriptions contain relative links like `file_activity`
that reference other classes. These become broken links when rendered
from nested directory paths. External links (http/https) are preserved
as markdown; internal class references are converted to plain text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

OCSF reference docs, sidebar, and version config are now fully
conditional. Build succeeds without running generate:ocsf, and the
OCSF section only appears in sidebar when generated content exists.
Generated content files are now gitignored.

Also fix broken cross-reference links in OCSF content by properly
converting object/class names with path separators to URL slugs.

commit.message:
Improve OCSF documentation generation and cross-referencing

Changes to class and object documentation:
- Cross-link object types in attributes (e.g., device → /reference/ocsf/1-7-0/objects/device)
- Simplify type display from `object` (device) to just [device](...)
- Move Profiles section after Attributes for better document flow
- Deduplicate "Objects Used" by using Set to track unique objects

Changes to profile documentation:
- Add "Available In" section showing which classes use each profile
- Support profile usage tracking and cross-linking

Refactoring:
- Extract buildProfileUsage() helper function
- Update formatType() to accept optional versionSlug for linking
- Simplify main OCSF index page layout and wording

commit.message:
Fix crashes during gRPC operator shutdown (#5661)

## Summary

Fixed critical concurrency bugs in three gRPC-based operators that
caused SIGSEGV crashes, assertion failures, and heap corruption.

## Changes

**from_google_cloud_pubsub:** Fixed two issues:
1. **Data race:** The subscription callback and main loop were both
accessing the `multi_series_builder` concurrently. The callback held the
mutex while adding records, but `yield_ready_as_table_slice()` was
called without the mutex. This caused assertion failures (`length() ==
old_length - count`) and heap corruption in jemalloc. Fixed by holding
the mutex while collecting slices, then yielding outside the critical
section.
2. **Use-after-free:** `session.cancel()` is async, so gRPC threads
could still be executing callbacks after the operator returns. Fixed by
always calling `session.get()` after `session.cancel()` to block until
the session is fully stopped.

**to_google_cloud_pubsub:** In-flight publish operations were abandoned
if timeouts occurred, leaving the publisher to be destroyed while gRPC
operations were still active. Fixed by calling `publisher.Flush()`
before the operator completes.

**from_velociraptor:** The completion queue was destroyed without being
shut down, and `Finish()` was called with a `nullptr` tag making it
impossible to wait for completion. Fixed by using a proper tag for
`Finish()`, waiting for it via the completion queue, calling
`Shutdown()`, and draining remaining events.

## Impact

These fixes eliminate production crashes that occur during
high-throughput gRPC operations or pipeline shutdown:
- SIGSEGV (signal 11, NULL pointer dereference)
- Assertion failures in series builder
- Heap corruption in jemalloc

## Testing

- Build completes successfully
- Code follows established patterns (mutex for concurrent access,
session.get() after cancel, Flush() before destruction, Shutdown() +
drain loop)
- No existing unit tests (external services required); recommend
ASAN/TSAN builds for additional coverage

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Simplify Velociraptor gRPC shutdown logic

Shut down the completion queue immediately after calling Finish, then
drain all remaining events in a single loop instead of waiting for
the Finish tag first and then draining separately.

commit.message:
Fix data race in from_google_cloud_pubsub

The subscription callback and main loop were both accessing the
multi_series_builder concurrently without proper synchronization.
The callback held the mutex while adding records, but the main loop
called yield_ready_as_table_slice() without the mutex, causing:

- Assertion failures: `length() == old_length - count`
- Heap corruption in jemalloc during protobuf operations
- SIGSEGV crashes

Fixed by holding the mutex while collecting slices from the builder,
then yielding them outside the critical section to avoid holding the
lock during coroutine suspension.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

The callback held the mutex while adding records, but the main loop
called yield_ready_as_table_slice() without the mutex, causing:

- Assertion failures: `length() == old_length - count`
- Heap corruption in jemalloc during protobuf operations
- SIGSEGV crashes

Fixed by holding the mutex while collecting slices from the builder,
then yielding them outside the critical section to avoid holding the
lock during coroutine suspension.

commit.message:
Fix crashes during gRPC operator shutdown

Fixed critical use-after-free bugs in `from_google_cloud_pubsub`,
`to_google_cloud_pubsub`, and `from_velociraptor` operators that caused
SIGSEGV crashes during shutdown.

The root cause was gRPC background threads continuing to access destroyed
resources after operator completion. The fixes ensure proper synchronization:

- `from_google_cloud_pubsub`: Always wait for subscription session to fully
  stop via session.get() after cancel() to block until gRPC threads finish.
- `to_google_cloud_pubsub`: Call publisher.Flush() before destruction to
  ensure all pending publishes complete.
- `from_velociraptor`: Properly shutdown the completion queue and drain
  remaining events before destruction.

These changes eliminate NULL pointer dereferences that could occur during
pipeline shutdown, particularly in production with high-throughput gRPC.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix crashes during gRPC operator shutdown

Fixed critical use-after-free bugs in `from_google_cloud_pubsub`,
`to_google_cloud_pubsub`, and `from_velociraptor` operators that caused
SIGSEGV crashes during shutdown.

The root cause was gRPC background threads continuing to access destroyed
resources after operator completion. The fixes ensure proper synchronization:

- `from_google_cloud_pubsub`: Always wait for subscription session to fully
  stop via session.get() after cancel() to block until gRPC threads finish.
- `to_google_cloud_pubsub`: Call publisher.Flush() before destruction to
  ensure all pending publishes complete.
- `from_velociraptor`: Properly shutdown the completion queue and drain
  remaining events before destruction.

These changes eliminate NULL pointer dereferences that could occur during

author Matthias Vallentin <matthias@vallentin.net> 1768394472 +0100
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1768394472 +0100

Fix stack-use-after-return in generator coroutine with Apple Clang (#5657)

## Summary

Fixes a stack-use-after-return bug in `generator.hpp` by adopting the
C++23 standard awaiter pattern (P2502/libstdc++) for handling lvalue
yields. This replaces the earlier P2529R0 "Owner struct" approach that
was susceptible to compiler bugs.

## Problem

When building Tenzir with Apple Clang 17 (Xcode toolchain),
AddressSanitizer reports use-after-free when the binary starts up:

```
ERROR: AddressSanitizer: stack-use-after-return
READ of size 8 at 0x00016dc05090 thread T0
#0 tenzir::internal::generator_promise<...>::value()
```

## Root Cause: Evolution of Best Practices

### P2529R0 Pattern (Early, Problematic)

The original cppcoro code and early P2529R0 proposal used an "Owner
struct" pattern:

```cpp
auto yield_value(const T& v) {
  struct Owner : std::suspend_always {
    Owner(const T& val, Pointer& out) : v(val) { out = &v; }  // Pointer set in constructor
    T v;
  };
  return Owner(v, m_value);  // Temporary may be destroyed too early
}
```

This assumes temporaries have their lifetime extended through
`await_resume()` per C++23 [expr.await]. However, this is a known
category of LLVM/Clang bugs (Issues #56301, #64945, #77482;
Differentials D82314, D89066, D99227).

### C++23 Standard Pattern (Current Best Practice)

The accepted C++23 `std::generator` (P2502, implemented in libstdc++)
uses a proper awaiter where **the pointer is set in `await_suspend()`**,
not before suspension:

```cpp
struct copy_awaiter {
  T value;
  Pointer& out;
  
  bool await_ready() noexcept { return false; }
  void await_suspend(handle) noexcept {
    out = std::addressof(value);  // Set DURING suspension, when address is stable
  }
  void await_resume() noexcept {}
};
```

This ensures the awaiter is fully constructed and its address is stable
before storing the pointer.

## Solution

Implemented the C++23 standard awaiter pattern:

```cpp
template <typename T, typename Pointer>
struct copy_awaiter {
  T value;
  Pointer& out;

  constexpr bool await_ready() const noexcept { return false; }
  
  template <typename Promise>
  constexpr void await_suspend(stdcoro::coroutine_handle<Promise>) noexcept {
    out = std::addressof(value);
  }
  
  constexpr void await_resume() const noexcept {}
};
```

The `yield_value` overload for lvalues now returns this awaiter:

```cpp
auto yield_value(const T& v)
  requires(!std::is_reference_v<T> && std::copy_constructible<T>)
{
  return copy_awaiter<value_type, pointer_type>{value_type(v), m_value};
}
```

## Research References

### C++ Standard Papers
- **P2502**: std::generator proposal (final C++23 standard)
- **P2529R0**: Early "generator should have T&& reference_type" proposal
- **LWG 3899**: Optimization for co_yielding lvalue generators

### LLVM Bug Reports
- **D82314, D89066, D99227**: Differential reviews for coroutine
lifetime fixes
- **Issue #56301**: Temporary object destroyed too early in coroutines
- **Issue #64945**: Coroutine temporaries not properly lifetime-extended
- **Issue #77482**: Additional coroutine temporary lifetime issues
- **`[[clang::coro_lifetimebound]]`**: Annotation added to detect these
patterns

### Implementation References
- libstdc++ `std::generator` uses `_Copy_awaiter` with pointer set in
`await_suspend`
- cppreference documents the "unspecified awaitable type" pattern

## Testing

- [x] Build with `macos-xcode-release` preset (Apple Clang 17)
- [x] Binary runs without ASan crash
- [x] Integration tests pass (590/593, 3 skipped)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Remove dead experimental coroutine fallback

<experimental/coroutine> branch can never be taken. Simplify to
just include the standard <coroutine> header.

Fix coroutine generator lvalue yield using C++23 standard pattern

Follow the C++23 standard awaiter pattern (P2502/libstdc++) for handling
lvalue yields in our coroutine generator. The key difference from the
earlier P2529R0 "Owner struct" approach is that the value pointer is now
set in await_suspend() rather than before suspension.

## Background

When yielding an lvalue to a generator<T>, a copy must be made and kept
alive until the yield is consumed. The original cppcoro implementation
and early P2529R0 proposal used an "Owner struct" pattern where:

1. yield_value() creates a struct that owns a copy of the value
2. The struct's constructor stores a pointer to its member
3. The struct inherits from suspend_always

This approach assumes temporaries in co_yield expressions have their
lifetime extended through await_resume() per C++23 [expr.await]. However,
some compilers (notably Apple Clang) have bugs where the temporary awaiter
is destroyed too early, causing use-after-free.

## The C++23 Standard Pattern

The accepted C++23 std::generator implementation (P2502, implemented in
libstdc++) uses a proper awaiter with:

- await_ready() returning false
- await_suspend() setting the value pointer (when address is stable)
- await_resume() doing nothing

This ensures the awaiter is fully constructed and its address is stable
before storing the pointer, avoiding the compiler bugs.

## Implementation

Introduced `copy_awaiter<T, Pointer>` in the internal namespace that:
- Stores a copy of the yielded value
- Sets the promise's value pointer in await_suspend()
- Has proper awaiter interface methods

The previous fix stored the copy in the promise via std::optional, which
worked but didn't follow the standard pattern. This update aligns with
how libstdc++ implements std::generator, which is the authoritative
reference for C++23 coroutine generators.

## References

- P2502: std::generator proposal (final C++23)
- P2529R0: generator should have T&& reference_type (early design)
- LWG 3899: co_yielding elements of an lvalue generator optimization
- LLVM issues: #56301, #64945, #77482 (coroutine temporary lifetime bugs)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement tail operator for new executor (#5663)

## Summary

- Implements the `tail` operator using the new `Operator<Input, Output>`
executor API
- Documents executor API invariants and lifecycle in `async.hpp` 
- Adds executor reference to the `developing-tenzir` skill

## Design

The `tail` operator uses a **buffering pattern**: it cannot emit output
until
end-of-stream because it needs to see all input to determine the "last
N" rows.
Key implementation details:

- Buffers table slices in `process()`, trimming front as rows accumulate
- Emits final output in `finalize()` when upstream closes
- Only snapshots mutable state (`buffer_`, `buffered_rows_`); `count_`
is
  immutable and restored from arguments
- Does not override `state()` since it never terminates early

## Test plan

- [ ] Build passes
- [ ] `from {} | enumerate | head 20 | tail 5` returns events 15-19
- [ ] `tail` (default) returns last 10 events

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Implement tail operator using new executor API

Ports the tail operator to use the new Operator<Input, Output> pattern.
The operator buffers all input and emits the last N rows in finalize(),
which is necessary since the "last N" cannot be determined until the
stream ends. The buffering is optimized to trim front rows as they
accumulate, avoiding unbounded memory growth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix macOS build with Homebrew-installed dependencies

Homebrew packages are built with Release configuration only, so their
CMake configs don't export IMPORTED_LOCATION for other configurations.
This caused the linker to emit bare library names like `-lgflags_shared`
instead of full paths, resulting in link failures.

Fix this with two complementary approaches:

1. Set CMAKE_MAP_IMPORTED_CONFIG on macOS to use Release libraries as
   fallback for Debug/RelWithDebInfo builds. Also map to empty string
   to handle packages that only set IMPORTED_LOCATION without config
   suffix.

2. Explicitly find gflags before folly since folly's CMake config
   references gflags_shared in INTERFACE_LINK_LIBRARIES. Manually set
   the missing IMPORTED_LOCATION_* properties for non-Release configs.

This enables building with `macos-xcode-relwithdebinfo` and similar
non-Release presets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

verification.payload:

parent 518baa26a2d5793b0dfc3807a121bc0156b24659
author Matthias Vallentin <matthias@vallentin.net> 1768472155 +0100
committer Matthias Vallentin <matthias@vallentin.net> 1768472155 +0100

Homebrew packages are built with Release configuration only, so their

This caused the linker to emit bare library names like `-lgflags_shared`
instead of full paths, resulting in link failures.

Fix this with two complementary approaches:

1. Set CMAKE_MAP_IMPORTED_CONFIG on macOS to use Release libraries as
   fallback for Debug/RelWithDebInfo builds. Also map to empty string
   to handle packages that only set IMPORTED_LOCATION without config
   suffix.

2. Explicitly find gflags before folly since folly's CMake config
   references gflags_shared in INTERFACE_LINK_LIBRARIES. Manually set
   the missing IMPORTED_LOCATION_* properties for non-Release configs.

This enables building with `macos-xcode-relwithdebinfo` and similar
non-Release presets.

commit.message:
Improve preview comment styling

Use a compact single-line format with status indicators:
- Live: 📦 **Preview** · [View →](url) · 🟢 Live

commit.message:
Migrate from pnpm to bun package manager

Replace pnpm with bun as the package manager for the documentation site.
This includes updating:

- package.json scripts: all `pnpm` commands now use `bun run`
- GitHub Actions workflows: use oven-sh/setup-bun instead of npm/pnpm setup
- Lock file: replaced pnpm-lock.yaml with bun.lock
- Build scripts: updated postinstall and generation scripts for bun
- Documentation: updated all command examples to use bun

The starlight-llms-txt GitHub monorepo dependency required special
handling in the fix script since bun installs it differently than pnpm.
Also added explicit ajv@^8.17.1 dependency to resolve a peer dependency
issue with ajv-draft-04.

commit.message:
Add emoji reaction-based changelog workflow

Transform the changelog workflow from auto-commit to a suggestion-based
flow where users approve, reject, or modify entries via emoji reactions.
The system generates changelog suggestions as comments and users can
react with:

👍 Accept and commit
👎 Reject (no changelog needed)
😕 Regenerate with different content
🚀 Make more technical
👀 Make less technical
😄 Add more wit

Uses 1-minute polling to detect reactions and dispatches actions
accordingly. Leverages the existing /ship:add command for composability
and proper changelog file generation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add inline test inputs and local inputs directories

Tests can now place input data directly alongside test files using the
.input extension. The harness automatically sets TENZIR_INPUT when a
matching file exists.

Additionally, you can create inputs/ directories at any level in the test
hierarchy. The harness walks up from each test and uses the nearest inputs/
directory for TENZIR_INPUTS, with shadowing semantics where nearer
directories take precedence. This lets you organize shared test data close
to the tests that use it.

The resolution hierarchy for TENZIR_INPUTS is:
1. inputs: override in test frontmatter or test.yaml
2. Nearest inputs/ directory walking up from the test
3. Package-level tests/inputs/ directory
4. Project-level inputs/ directory (fallback)

All existing tests continue to work with the global inputs/ directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

verification.payload:
tree af6d8bcc2a124683770a7c4b50af75b3523d6e0d
parent 2242e8e69713c331c29014f77f9df368c5955ef8
author Matthias Vallentin <matthias@vallentin.net> 1768488054 +0100
committer Matthias Vallentin <matthias@vallentin.net> 1768488054 +0100

commit.message:
Add optional clang-format version pinning

Repositories can now add a `.clang-format-version` file specifying
the required clang-format major version. If present, the hook only
runs when the installed version matches; otherwise it skips with a
clear message. This keeps backward compatibility (format with any
version if no file is present) while allowing teams to opt into
version consistency.

Resolves: #7

commit.message:
Consolidate changelog comment building into shared script

Extract comment formatting into .github/scripts/build-changelog-comment.sh,
used by both changelog-adder and changelog-reactor actions. This ensures
consistent formatting and uses tenzir-ship's native type-specific headers
(🚀 Features, 🐛 Bug Fixes, 💥 Breaking Changes, 🔧 Changes).

Also fixes pending suggestion detection - the previous jq query used
`head -1` which truncated multi-line comment bodies.

The action now keeps the entry file until after posting the comment,
so tenzir-ship can render it directly.

commit.message:
Add ship release engineering suite with review and workflow commands

Add three new ship commands for release engineering workflows: /ship:prepare
for creating topic branches/worktrees, /ship:review for parallel code review
with six specialized reviewer agents, and /ship:finalize for creating
changelog entries and committing changes. Includes six new reviewer agents
covering UX, docs, tests, architecture, security, and consistency with
confidence-scored findings. Adds ship:reviewing-changes skill for review
methodology. Introduces git:committer agent for autonomous commit creation.

requiring a .prettierrc configuration file. This ensures

commit.message:
Make formatter hook project-aware with config detection

The hook now searches for formatter config files by walking up from
the edited file's directory. It only applies formatting if the
project's config is found, preventing wrong formatting rules from
being applied to projects using different tools.

Supports config detection for Biome, ESLint, and Prettier with
proper fallback chains for each file type.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Poll PyPI API for version availability before verification

Replace fixed 30-second sleep with a retry loop that polls the PyPI
API every 5 seconds until the expected version appears. This handles
CDN propagation delays gracefully instead of failing on slow updates.

commit.message:
Improve release publish UX for partial failures

The release publish command now displays a progress panel when a step
fails mid-workflow. Users see checkmarks for completed steps, an X for
the failed step, and circles for pending steps—making it clear where
to resume recovery.

This helps users understand which git operations (commit, tag, push)
succeeded before the failure, and which remaining steps need attention,
enabling safe recovery from transient failures like network issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve plan review hook output handling and verdict detection

Refine the plan review hooks to handle tool output more cleanly and detect
blocking verdicts more accurately. This addresses issues with banner noise in
review output, false positive verdict matches, and incomplete blocking logic.

Key changes:
- Separate stdout/stderr to eliminate tool banners from review output
- Pass prompts via stdin to avoid exposing huge arguments in ps output
- Block on both REVISE and BLOCK verdicts, not just BLOCK alone
- Use anchored regex patterns to avoid matching template text in prompts
- Rename PLAN_REVIEW_BLOCK_ON_P1 to PLAN_REVIEW_BLOCK for clarity
- Add comprehensive PreToolUse hook documentation explaining limitations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

parent de217e330cecaa0601f52d96277a742aee38e686

commit.message:
Add plan review plugin for automated implementation plan evaluation

Introduce the plan review plugin that automatically evaluates implementation
plans using external AI tools when exiting plan mode. The plugin supports
multiple review tools (Codex, Gemini, or CLI-based), runs reviews in parallel,
and reports findings on a P1-P4 severity scale.

Components:
- hooks/hooks.json: Hook configuration for plan mode enter/exit
- hooks/notify-plan-review.sh: Notification on entering plan mode
- hooks/review-plan.sh: Review execution on exiting plan mode
- prompts/review.txt: Evaluation prompt template
- README.md: User documentation

Also adds .plans/ to .gitignore.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Refine review workflow with structured hook outputs

The review command's hook scripts now output structured control signals
that eliminate ambiguity about what commands to run and where to write
findings. The scope detection hook outputs `Scope:`, `Diff:`, and file
list, while the directory creation hook outputs `Review directory:` with
the path.

Hook documentation now includes design principles clarifying that hooks
should output all computed values the model needs, rather than requiring
the model to construct them. The review command documentation shows
concrete examples of using these outputs, and the reviewer skill guidance
emphasizes focusing on changed code rather than unchanged portions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add two-dimensional severity and confidence ratings to code reviews

Enhance the review system with independent severity (P1-P4) and
confidence (0-100) dimensions for better issue prioritization.

The reviewing-changes skill now defines:
- Severity levels (P1=Critical, P2=Important, P3=Minor, P4=Trivial)
- Confidence scores measuring certainty the finding is real
- Examples showing how dimensions combine independently

The review command computes action emojis from both dimensions:
- 🔴 Act now: High-severity (P1-P2) with high confidence (80%+)
- 🟠 Investigate: Potentially critical or confirmed minor issues
- 🟡 Consider: Lower-confidence moderate or confirmed trivial issues
- ⚪ Optional: Low-confidence trivial suggestions

This replaces the previous single-dimension system where severity was
implied from confidence, enabling more nuanced assessment of "how bad
is this?" versus "how sure am I?"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Preserve original field order in ocsf::derive

The ocsf::derive operator now preserves the original field order from input
records instead of reordering them alphabetically. This uses a two-phase
approach: pre-compute derived enum/sibling values, then emit fields in their
original input order.

This improves performance from O(n+s+f log f) to O(n+s) by eliminating the
alphabetical sort, while providing deterministic and predictable output.

Enum/sibling pair behavior:
- When both exist: each is emitted at its original position
- When only one exists: the derived counterpart is inserted immediately after
- Enum lists continue to pass through unchanged (not supported yet)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Migrate from ESLint to Biome for faster linting

Replace ESLint with Biome to improve linting performance. This change:
- Removes ESLint and related TypeScript plugins
- Adds Biome configuration with overrides for build scripts and Astro files
- Disables noConsole for scripts/ (run at build time, not production)
- Disables noExplicitAny for .astro files (pragmatic for component prop maps)
- Auto-fixes all lint issues: Node.js imports, template literals, optional chaining
- Updates lint scripts in package.json
- Restricts Prettier to markdown files only

Biome's unified configuration and faster execution improve developer workflow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commands (`/docs:write`, `/docs:review`, `/docs:pr`) with a two-phase

repository if missing, while subsequent operations perform staleness
checks. Also simplify command documentation with declarative language
instead of explicit bash code blocks.

commit.message:
Fix plan review hook to skip on user rejection

The PostToolUse hook for ExitPlanMode was displaying the "Starting
plan review..." notification even when plans were rejected. This caused
unnecessary tool invocations and confusing output.

The hook now checks tool_response for plan content before proceeding.
When a plan is rejected, the review is skipped silently. Only approved
plans trigger the external review tools.

Also removes the unused PreToolUse notification hook that was
superseded by this logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Refactor review workflow with synthesis and structured finding IDs

Replace raw finding display with intelligent synthesis using orchestrator
context. The orchestrator now deduplicates overlapping findings from multiple
reviewers, correlates related issues, and filters noise—giving users a focused
summary instead of an exhaustive dump.

Introduce structured issue IDs (SEC-1, ARC-1, etc.) for consistent tracking.
Raise the evidence bar upstream: reviewers must provide reasoning and concrete

commit.message:
Implement robust octet count validation in parse_syslog

Simplify the RFC 6587 octet-counting algorithm with clear mode distinction:

- Explicit mode (octet_counting=true): Trust the count, truncate if needed
- Auto mode (default): Treat prefix as hint, try full parse first

Algorithm:
1. Parse octet prefix if octet_counting != false
2. If explicit and prefix missing/invalid → warn, null
3. If prefix found:
   a. actual < stated → warn, null (incomplete)
   b. actual == stated → parse content
   c. actual > stated:
      - explicit → truncate, parse, warn
      - auto → try full first, fall back to truncated
4. No prefix → parse full input
5. Parse failure → warn, null
6. Emit parsed message

Refactored to use helper lambdas (try_parse, add_parsed, add_null) to
eliminate code duplication.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Preserve original field order in ocsf::derive (#5673)

## Summary

The `ocsf::derive` operator now preserves the original field order from
input records instead of reordering them alphabetically.

## Changes

- **Algorithm**: Restructured to iterate over input fields in their
original order instead of OCSF schema fields
- **Two-phase approach**:
  1. Pre-compute all derived enum/sibling values upfront
  2. Emit fields in their original input order
- **Removed**: The `std::ranges::sort()` call that alphabetically sorted
output fields
- **Performance**: Improved from O(n+s+f log f) to O(n+s)

## Behavior

| Scenario | Output |
|----------|--------|
| Both enum and sibling in input | Each at its original position |
| Only enum (int) | Enum, then derived sibling immediately after |
| Only sibling (string) | Derived enum immediately before, then sibling
|
| Non-OCSF fields | At their original position |

## Test Updates

All 13 `ocsf::derive` integration tests updated to reflect new field
ordering. All 67 OCSF tests pass.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Add RFC 6587 octet-counting support to parse_syslog

The `parse_syslog` function now supports RFC 6587 octet-counted framing
via the new `octet_counting` parameter:

- Not specified: auto-detect and strip length prefix if valid
- `octet_counting=true`: require prefix, warn if missing
- `octet_counting=false`: never strip prefix

The implementation introduces `octet_length_parser`, a shared parser
combinator used by both `parse_syslog` (single-value) and `read_syslog`
(streaming) to ensure consistent behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Isolate .docs/ clones across git worktrees in docs plugin

The docs plugin now properly isolates documentation repository clones
across different git worktrees, preventing the docs:writer agent from
discovering and using another worktree's .docs/ directory. Three key
improvements: init hooks run on first tool call (using * matcher),
synchronize-docs.sh blocks with actionable messages on clone failure,
and writer.md includes explicit guidance to prevent agent confusion.

commit.message:
Add dynamic documentation loading for tenzir plugin

Introduce the tenzir:docs skill that automatically syncs from the latest
GitHub release, providing current documentation without requiring plugin
updates. The sync process uses a 24-hour cache and dual strategies:
non-blocking background sync at session start and blocking sync when the
skill is accessed, ensuring optimal performance and freshness.

commit.message:
Merge ship plugin into dev plugin

Consolidate ship plugin functionality into dev to create a unified developer
utilities plugin. The dev plugin now provides documentation workflows,
changelog management, code review, and release engineering all in one place.

Changes:
- Move changelog-adder agent (from add.md) to dev with embedded logic
- Move 7 specialized code reviewers (arch, docs, performance, readability,
  security, tests, ux) to dev/agents/reviewers/
- Move review, finalize, and release commands to dev/commands/
- Move review helper scripts and reviewing-changes skill to dev
- Update all external references in GitHub Actions workflows and configs
- Remove prepare functionality (no longer needed)
- Delete ship plugin entirely
- Bump dev plugin to 3.0.0 (major version for breaking changes)

Users now access:
- @dev:changelog-adder for changelog entry creation
- @dev:reviewers:* for specialized code review agents
- /dev:review to spawn all reviewers in parallel
- /dev:finalize for changelog + commit + push
- /dev:release for guided release workflows

commit.message:
Fix changelog entry ordering in multi-project show command (#6)

* Fix changelog entry ordering in multi-project show command

Entries were displayed newest-first instead of oldest-first when
showing multiple projects. Changed the sort key in
_render_entries_multi_project() from -ts to ts to sort entries
ascending by date, ensuring consistency with single-project display
and placing the newest entry at the bottom of the table where users
expect it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Ensure consistent entry ordering in multi-project show command

The multi-project show command now displays entries in chronological
order (oldest first), matching single-project behavior and user
expectations. Previously entries were sorted newest-first, creating
an inconsistency in the application's sorting behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix release recovery instructions showing placeholder branch names (#5)

* Fix release recovery instructions showing placeholder branch names

When a release fails during the push_branch step, recovery
instructions showed 'git push origin <branch>:<branch>' instead of
the actual branch name. Extract branch resolution logic into a new
get_push_branch_info() function that returns push info without
executing, allowing the step tracker to display accurate commands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Update tenzir-plugins: Fix phantom pipeline entries (#5680)

## Summary

- Update tenzir-plugins submodule to include fix for phantom pipeline
entries with empty IDs

## Related

- Companion PR: https://github.com/tenzir/tenzir-plugins/pull/480

## Background

Several places in the pipeline manager used `stored_pipelines[id]`
without checking if the entry exists first. This could create phantom
entries when async callbacks ran after the original pipeline was
deleted, or when pipelines with empty IDs were loaded from persisted
state.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Adapt tenzir-test invocations to use environment variables (#5682)

## Summary

Switches all tenzir-test invocations from CLI arguments to environment
variables for specifying binary paths. This adapts the codebase to the
recent removal of CLI argument support in tenzir-test, which now uses
environment variables exclusively:

- `TENZIR_BINARY` (environment variable) replaces `--tenzir-binary` (CLI
argument)
- `TENZIR_NODE_BINARY` (environment variable) replaces
`--tenzir-node-binary` (CLI argument)

## Files Updated

- **Dockerfile**: Updated the integration test stage to pass binary
paths via environment variables
- **nix/tenzir/check.nix**: Updated Nix build configuration to use
environment variables
- **tenzir/CMakeLists.txt**: Updated CMake build system test command
generation to use environment variables

## Test Plan

- Build Tenzir using the updated CMakeLists.txt configuration
- Run integration tests in Docker to verify the environment variables
are properly passed
- Verify Nix builds complete successfully with the updated check.nix
- Confirm tenzir-test correctly receives binary paths via environment
variables

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Add RFC 6587 octet-counting support to parse_syslog (#5674)

## Summary

The `parse_syslog` function now supports RFC 6587 octet-counted framing,
where syslog messages are prefixed with their byte length (for example,
`65 <syslog-message>`). This framing is commonly used in TCP-based
syslog transport to handle message boundaries.

## Changes

- Add `octet_counting` parameter to `parse_syslog` with three modes:
- **Not specified (default)**: Auto-detect and strip length prefix if
present and valid
- **`octet_counting=true`**: Require a length prefix, emit warning if
missing
  - **`octet_counting=false`**: Never strip a length prefix
- Introduce `octet_length_parser`, a shared parser combinator used by
both `parse_syslog` (single-value) and `read_syslog` (streaming) for
consistent behavior
- Add integration tests covering all three modes

## Design decisions

- Auto-detection validates that the stated length matches the remaining
content exactly, preventing false positives where input coincidentally
starts with `<digits><space>`
- Uses `uint32_t` for the length field per RFC 6587's grammar
(`NONZERO-DIGIT *DIGIT`)

## Test plan

- [x] Manual testing of all three `octet_counting` modes
- [x] Integration tests pass (4 new test cases)
- [x] Build succeeds

## Related PRs

- Docs: https://github.com/tenzir/docs/pull/167

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

  - **`octet_counting=false`**: Never strip a length prefix
- Introduce `octet_length_parser`, a shared parser combinator used by
both `parse_syslog` (single-value) and `read_syslog` (streaming) for
consistent behavior
- Add integration tests covering all three modes

## Design decisions

- Auto-detection validates that the stated length matches the remaining
content exactly, preventing false positives where input coincidentally
starts with `<digits><space>`
- Uses `uint32_t` for the length field per RFC 6587's grammar
(`NONZERO-DIGIT *DIGIT`)

## Test plan

- [x] Manual testing of all three `octet_counting` modes
- [x] Integration tests pass (4 new test cases)
- [x] Build succeeds

## Related PRs

- Docs: https://github.com/tenzir/docs/pull/167

specific finding without accumulated context from previous fixes.

commit.message:
Add /fix command for iterative finding fixes

New command works through review findings one-by-one, creating a commit for
each fix. For GitHub findings, it replies to threads with the commit SHA and
resolves them automatically.

This restores the iterative workflow lost when /address-pr-comments was
converted to a reviewer agent. The new design generalizes to all reviewer
findings, not just GitHub comments.

Changes:
- Add /fix command (plugins/dev/commands/fix.md)
- Update /review step 6 to offer both /fix and plan mode
- Add thread resolution to plan mode path (step 5)

commit.message:
Add hunk-based formatting for C++ files in format hook

The format hook now applies clang-format to only the changed hunks
in staged C++ files rather than the entire file. This keeps git
history cleaner by preventing unrelated formatting changes from
appearing in commits.

commit.message:
Refactor plan review from hooks to skill and agent

Plan review functionality has been converted from hook-based scripts
into a structured skill and agent. The new `reviewing-plans` skill
provides a formal methodology for evaluating plans across five weighted
dimensions (completeness, correctness, feasibility, risk, clarity)
with consistent P1/P2 severity levels. The `plan-reviewer` agent
exposes this as a reusable workflow. Hook-based review triggers
(EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode) have been removed in favor of this more
robust, composable approach.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Auto-detect Tenzir binary with uvx fallback (#7)

* Auto-detect Tenzir binary with uvx fallback

Replace CLI flags with automatic binary detection: TENZIR_BINARY environment
variable takes precedence, PATH lookup follows, and uvx is the final fallback.
This removes --tenzir-binary and --tenzir-node-binary flags as a breaking
change to encourage using environment variables instead. Binary representation
changes from str|None to tuple[str,...]|None to support multi-part commands
like ("uvx", "tenzir").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add changelog entry for auto-detect Tenzir binary

Documents the breaking change removing --tenzir-binary and
--tenzir-node-binary CLI flags in favor of environment variables
with automatic uvx fallback detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Implement reviewer changes

* Fix type annotations for binary environment variables

Update type annotations in state.py to reflect that TENZIR_BINARY
and TENZIR_NODE_BINARY are tuples of command parts, not strings. Also
update test_run_config.py to use shlex.join() when comparing binary
environment variables and fix test fixtures to pass tuples instead of
strings to match the new command structure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix uvx fallback for tenzir-node

Use `uvx --from tenzir tenzir-node` since tenzir-node is not a
standalone package but a script within the tenzir package.

commit.message:
Make test output verbosity opt-in with --verbose flag (#6)

* Make test output verbosity opt-in with --verbose flag

The new --verbose/-v flag makes detailed per-test output opt-in. By
default, quiet mode only shows failures and a compact summary,
significantly reducing noise for large test suites. Passthrough mode
automatically enables verbose output to preserve output ordering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add changelog entry for verbose output flag

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Document verbose flag behavior and add comprehensive tests

Add docstrings and help text explaining that verbose mode controls both
pass/skip output while failures always print. Clarify that verbose is
automatically enabled in passthrough mode. Update help text to show that
by default only failures are displayed.

Replace direct access to VERBOSE_OUTPUT module variable with is_verbose_output()
getter in tests. Add two new test cases verifying that success and skip
messages are suppressed when verbose mode is disabled.

commit.message:
Add orphaned directory cleanup for extra_paths sync

When files/directories are removed from a source repository, they weren't
being removed from this news repository during sync. This happened because
glob patterns in extra_paths only expand to directories that exist in the
source, and rsync --delete only removes files within synced directories.

Add a cleanup step that compares destination subdirectories against the
source and removes any that no longer exist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

verification.payload:
tree 00f44d7dbdebf3fb94cf8617a1da0c30e054cf47
parent ebea36efe64bccbfbcadc65bf3781105044ec7a5

committer Matthias Vallentin <matthias@vallentin.net> 1769179057 +0100

Add orphaned directory cleanup for extra_paths sync

When files/directories are removed from a source repository, they weren't
being removed from this news repository during sync. This happened because
glob patterns in extra_paths only expand to directories that exist in the
source, and rsync --delete only removes files within synced directories.

Add a cleanup step that compares destination subdirectories against the
source and removes any that no longer exist.

Query Log events to OCSF DNS Activity (class 4003). Supports cloud profile

dispositions. Includes test data and expected output.

commit.message:
Migrate starlight-llms-txt fork to local plugin implementation (#172)

* Migrate starlight-llms-txt fork to local plugin implementation

Remove dependency on the external starlight-llms-txt fork and replace
it with a minimal local plugin implementation that contains only the
features we actually use: projectName, description, perPageMarkdown,
customSets, and demote. This simplifies maintenance and eliminates the
need for the postinstall script fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix starlight-llms-txt plugin review findings

Addresses security, documentation, readability, and code quality issues:

**Security (P2):**
- Escape regex special characters in locale keys to prevent ReDoS attacks
- Add try-catch for JSON serialization with descriptive error messages

**Documentation (P2):**
- Document LLM Documentation feature in CLAUDE.md, including generation flags
  and output formats

**UX (P2):**
- Improve error message for missing site configuration with concrete example

**Readability (P3):**
- Rename `tree` to `fileTree` to avoid shadowing loop variable
- Extract sorting formula into semantic variables (promotionRank, demotionPenalty)
- Add optional chaining for safe property access on file data

**Code Quality (P3):**
- Fix JSDoc typo in CustomSetUserConfig
- Correct plugin name from "text" to "txt" in vite plugin registration

commit.message:
Fix uninitialized sys_ pointer in base_ctx 2-argument constructor

The 2-argument base_ctx constructor left sys_ uninitialized, causing
undefined behavior when code later attempts implicit conversion to
caf::actor_system&. This manifests as a runtime assertion failure when
TENZIR_ASSERT(sys_) fires in the system() method.

The issue was discovered via test failures in:
  tests/compiler/finalize/export_where.tql

Stack trace shows the access path:
  make_where_ir (ir.cpp) → where->compile → ... → base_ctx::system()

The root cause is that make_where_ir creates a compile_ctx without an
actor_system, but some code path in the where operator compilation
attempts to access it. Initializing sys_ to nullptr makes the failure
deterministic (assertion with clear error) instead of undefined behavior.

TODO: Investigate why actor_system is accessed during IR optimization
and either:
  a) Pass actor_system to make_where_ir, or
  b) Remove the actor_system dependency from the compile path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

parent f244d355e051ac0f03b529e1629dc1d7e9fd1460
author Matthias Vallentin <matthias@vallentin.net> 1769459667 +0100
committer Matthias Vallentin <matthias@vallentin.net> 1769459667 +0100

Fix uninitialized sys_ pointer in base_ctx 2-argument constructor

The 2-argument base_ctx constructor left sys_ uninitialized, causing

The issue was discovered via test failures in:

Stack trace shows the access path:
  make_where_ir (ir.cpp) → where->compile → ... → base_ctx::system()

The root cause is that make_where_ir creates a compile_ctx without an
actor_system, but some code path in the where operator compilation

TODO: Investigate why actor_system is accessed during IR optimization

commit.message:
Force static folly on all platforms with fPIC for shared builds

Force folly to build as a static library for two reasons:

1. On macOS, std::current_exception() returns nullptr in coroutine
   unhandled_exception() handlers when folly crosses shared library
   boundaries.

2. On Linux aarch64, folly's CMake has duplicate aor (Arm Optimized
   Routines) object targets that cause multiple definition errors
   when building as a shared library.

When the main build uses shared libraries (like Docker), folly must
be compiled with -fPIC even though it's a static library, because
it will be linked into a shared libtenzir.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

verification.payload:
tree 19cc26619f5adaa988addd9866ce1a6efba194ff
parent 721691a82e186983392d3949456acac8e26ca748
author Matthias Vallentin <matthias@vallentin.net> 1769450878 +0100
committer Matthias Vallentin <matthias@vallentin.net> 1769453406 +0100

commit.message:
Instantiate implicit sink before spawning

The implicit sink (e.g., `write_tql color=true`) was compiled but never
instantiated, leaving argument expressions as `Incomplete`. This caused
a crash when `--color=always` was used because the `color=true` argument
was not evaluated before `spawn()` was called.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

committer Matthias Vallentin <matthias@vallentin.net> 1769446759 +0100

Instantiate implicit sink before spawning

The implicit sink (e.g., `write_tql color=true`) was compiled but never
instantiated, leaving argument expressions as `Incomplete`. This caused
a crash when `--color=always` was used because the `color=true` argument
was not evaluated before `spawn()` was called.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Build OCSF schemas locally instead of using remote API (#174)

* Build OCSF schemas locally instead of using remote API

Replace flaky schema.ocsf.io API dependency with local builds using the
official ocsf-server Elixir project. Improves reliability, caches
dependencies, and skips OCSF generation in PR builds to save CI time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix YAML heredoc parsing by extracting Elixir script to separate file

The heredoc content starting at column 1 within a YAML `run: |` block
caused YAML parsing errors. Extract the Elixir export script to a
separate file and reference it via $GITHUB_ACTION_PATH.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Consolidate /dev:review and /dev:fix into unified workflow

Merge the review and fix commands into a seamless 4-phase workflow:
Review → Triage → Plan → Execute. Both PR and batch modes now use task
lists as the interface between planning and execution phases.

Changes:
- review.md: Added orchestration logic for full workflow with phase gates
- New triager agent: Filters and groups findings by impact and codebase area
- New planner agent: Creates fix task list with dependencies and prioritization
- New addressing-reviews skill: GitHub PR communication best practices
- fixer.md: Extended to support grouped findings with cross-references
- detect-pr-context.sh: New hook for PR vs batch mode detection
- Removed fix.md: Functionality now integrated into review workflow

This unification reduces tool switching and provides clearer task
management for complex finding portfolios.

committer Matthias Vallentin <matthias@vallentin.net> 1769424647 +0100

Consolidate /dev:review and /dev:fix into unified workflow

Merge the review and fix commands into a seamless 4-phase workflow:
Review → Triage → Plan → Execute. Both PR and batch modes now use task
lists as the interface between planning and execution phases.

Changes:
- review.md: Added orchestration logic for full workflow with phase gates
- New triager agent: Filters and groups findings by impact and codebase area
- New planner agent: Creates fix task list with dependencies and prioritization
- New addressing-reviews skill: GitHub PR communication best practices
- fixer.md: Extended to support grouped findings with cross-references
- detect-pr-context.sh: New hook for PR vs batch mode detection
- Removed fix.md: Functionality now integrated into review workflow

This unification reduces tool switching and provides clearer task
management for complex finding portfolios.

commit.message:
Add batch mode to /dev:fix for non-PR workflows

Introduce two fix modes based on PR scope detection:

- PR mode: Interactive per-finding confirmation, individual commits with
  push, GitHub thread resolution. Preserves existing behavior for PR
  review workflows.

- Batch mode: Autonomous processing after initial confirmation, no
  individual commits. Offers single summary commit at end. Better UX
  for local development and documentation fixes.

The fixer agent now accepts a Mode parameter (PR or Batch) and
conditionally skips commit/push/thread resolution in batch mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix overly broad version string detection in show command

The show command now correctly distinguishes between release versions and
changelog entry IDs that happen to start with 'v'. It no longer
misidentifies entries like 'v2-draft-feature' as release versions.

Changes:
- Compute known release versions once per invocation for performance
- Check against actual known versions instead of just 'v' prefix
- Use case-insensitive version matching while preserving case for display
- Add comprehensive tests for scope validation and case-insensitive handling

Fixes changelog entry ID detection when they contain version-like patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Discord notifications for string author values

Discord notifications were showing individual characters instead of the
author name when authors were specified as a string (e.g., `authors: lava`)
instead of a list. The manual YAML parsing didn't normalize this case.

Now using tenzir-ship's read_entry() API which properly normalizes author
and PR metadata to lists before processing, fixing the issue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
